title: SMS/MMS and Call Log Backup Schema
description: Schema for JSON output produced by sms-backup-restore-parser from SyncTech SMS Backup & Restore XML exports.

sms.description: Array of SMS message records extracted from the backup XML.

items.description: A single SMS message record. All values are strings, extracted directly from XML attributes via dict(elem.attrib).

protocol.description: Transport protocol identifier. Almost always "0" for standard SMS.

address.description: Phone number of the sender or recipient. Format varies by carrier and locale.

date.description: Timestamp as Java epoch milliseconds (milliseconds since 1970-01-01 00:00:00 UTC).

date_iso.description: Computed ISO 8601 UTC timestamp derived from the date field. Injected by the parser.

subject.description: Message subject line. Almost universally the literal string "null" for SMS.

body.description: Full text content of the message.

toa.description: Type of Address for the destination. Typically "null".

sc_toa.description: Type of Address for the service center. Typically "null".

service_center.description: SMSC number that routed the message. Present for received messages, typically "null" for sent.

read.description: Whether the message has been read. 0=Unread, 1=Read.

locked.description: Whether the message is locked (protected from deletion). 0=Unlocked, 1=Locked.

date_sent.description: Timestamp when the message was sent, in Java epoch milliseconds. May be "0" if unavailable.

sub_id.description: SIM subscription ID identifying which SIM slot handled the message. Format is device-dependent.

readable_date.description: Human-readable date string. Only present when the backup preference is enabled.

contact_name.description: Contact display name for the address. Only present when the backup preference is enabled. "(Unknown)" if no match.

mms.description: Array of MMS message records extracted from the backup XML.

items.description: A single MMS message record. Root-level attributes are strings from XML. Nested parts and addrs are collected into arrays.

date.description: Timestamp in Java epoch milliseconds for when the message was sent or received.

date_iso.description: Computed ISO 8601 UTC timestamp derived from the date field. Injected by the parser.

ct_t.description: Top-level Content-Type of the MMS message (e.g. "application/vnd.wap.multipart.related").

rr.description: Read report request flag. Indicates whether a read receipt was requested.

sub.description: Subject line of the MMS message, if provided by the sender.

read_status.description: Read status of the message in the MMS protocol sense.

address.description: Phone number of the primary sender or recipient.

m_id.description: Globally unique Message-ID assigned by the MMS service center.

read.description: Whether the user has read this message. 0=Unread, 1=Read.

m_size.description: Total size of the MMS message in bytes, as reported by the carrier.

m_type.description: MMS message type code per WAP MMS spec (e.g. 128=Send Request, 132=Retrieve Confirmation).

text_only.description: Flag indicating text-only MMS with no media. 0=Has media, 1=Text only.

retr_st.description: Retrieval status from the MMS server.

ct_cls.description: Content class of the MMS, defining the category of content allowed.

sub_cs.description: Character set encoding used for the subject field.

ct_l.description: Content-Location URI where the MMS content can be retrieved.

tr_id.description: Transaction ID used to correlate MMS protocol exchanges between client and server.

retr_txt_cs.description: Character set for the retrieval text.

d_rpt.description: Delivery report request flag. Indicates whether delivery confirmation was requested.

date_sent.description: Timestamp when the message was sent, in Java epoch milliseconds.

seen.description: Whether the message notification has been seen (distinct from read). 0=Not seen, 1=Seen.

v.description: MMS specification version number (e.g. "18" for version 1.2).

exp.description: Expiry duration or timestamp for the message on the MMS server, in seconds.

resp_txt.description: Response text from the MMS server.

locked.description: Whether the message is locked against deletion. 0=Unlocked, 1=Locked.

retr_txt.description: Retrieval text accompanying the message from the MMS server.

resp_st.description: Response status code from the MMS server.

sim_slot.description: SIM card slot identifier. Not in official XSD but appears in real backups.

readable_date.description: Human-readable date string. Only present when the backup preference is enabled.

contact_name.description: Contact display name for the address. Only present when the backup preference is enabled.

parts.description: Array of MMS part objects representing the multipart message components.

items.description: A single MMS part (text, image, audio, video, or SMIL layout descriptor).

seq.description: Sequence number controlling part order within the message. Parts are ordered by ascending seq value.

ct.description: MIME content type of this part (e.g. "text/plain", "image/jpeg", "application/smil").

name.description: Name of the part, often derived from the original filename. May be "null".

chset.description: Character set encoding for text parts. Uses IANA charset numbers (e.g. "106" for UTF-8).

cd.description: Content-Disposition header value. Indicates how the part should be presented.

fn.description: Filename for the part as specified in content headers. May differ from name.

cid.description: Content-ID used to reference this part from SMIL layout or other parts.

cl.description: Content-Location providing a URI-like identifier for the part.

ctt_s.description: Content-Type start parameter. Identifies the root part of the multipart message.

ctt_t.description: Content-Type type parameter. Specifies the overall type of the multipart message.

text.description: Text content of the part. Populated for text/plain parts, typically "null" for binary parts.

data.description: Base64-encoded binary payload. Present for media parts (images, audio, video). Primary driver of large file sizes.

addrs.description: Array of MMS address objects identifying participants in the conversation.

items.description: A single MMS address entry. For group MMS, multiple addr entries exist (one per participant).

address.description: Phone number or email address of this participant.

charset.description: Character set identifier for this address entry (e.g. "106" for UTF-8).

calls.description: Array of call log records extracted from the backup XML.

items.description: A single call log record. All values are strings, extracted directly from XML attributes.

number.description: Phone number associated with the call. May include country code prefixes.

duration.description: Length of the call in seconds. "0" for missed or rejected calls.

date.description: Timestamp of the call in Java epoch milliseconds.

date_iso.description: Computed ISO 8601 UTC timestamp derived from the date field. Injected by the parser.

subscription_id.description: SIM subscription that handled the call. Format varies: simple index or full SIM ICCID string.

readable_date.description: Human-readable date string. Only present when the backup preference is enabled.

contact_name.description: Contact display name matched to the number. Only present when the backup preference is enabled.
